title: Level 121
wikitext:
{{Headerbox|file=shoparc.png|header=Waiting is just the beginning...|speed=40s}} {{Class Custom Image|bg-image=backborder.png|class=Undetermined|hexagon=?|safety=Mysterious Properties|security=Unknown Information|entity=Undocumented Entities|text-color=#f3f3f3|bg-size=1600px}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 121: "Vacant Plaza"}} {{CSS|content= /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: Jost}}
[[File:backborder.png|2000px]]
{{Background|file=mallhm.png}} {{CSS|content= :root { --theme-page-background-color: #36352E; --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #57533D; --theme-page-text-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-community-header-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-link-color: #FFEFBA; .fandom-community-header { background-image: linear-gradient(transparent, var(--theme-page-background-color)); margin-bottom: -13px; }} '''Level 121''', also known as "The Mall" or "Vacant Plaza," is the 122nd level of the Backrooms.
Description
{{Blurimage|file=mallhm.png|caption=A photo of a dimly lit mall section.}} '''Level 121''' is a vacant shopping complex consisting of an infinite amount of interconnected floors. Its main architecture mimics the familiar layout of commercial shopping centers while displaying slight differences in ambience, which likely stems from a keyboard-like melody of unknown origin that can be heard playing faintly in the background regardless of a wanderer's location. The level's most prominent features are its sprawling central areas surrounded by countless levels of retail spaces, and dim corridors that stretch for hundreds of meters. While most storefronts appear closed down, a select number are still open, though most of the sold products have been reported to be either broken or harmful. Wanderers are advised to remain cautious when venturing inside stores. The construction materials identified inside the level, namely the nearly indestructible material composing its walls, remains unknown as previous efforts to isolate it have resulted in the material dissipating. Upon further testing, it has been proven that corrosive substances such as [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] can dissolve this material, though the process is extremely slow and takes around 4-5 hours to corrode just a small chunk of it. A dull-red carpet can occasionally be seen inside certain storefronts and emits a distinct, almond-like scent commonly associated with [[Level 0]]. Despite thorough exploration, no conventional exits have been documented, and the occasional window or door found displays a pitch-black void outside. Previous wanderers have also reported working technology despite the lack of electricity, such as the elevators or overhead lighting. Further investigation has revealed that this property seems limited to '''Level 121''', as these components will not work in other levels, prompting studies into the possible manipulation of this elusive force. '''Level 121''' also harbors 'mirrored' stores- identical shops that appear in multiple locations and reflect any changes made to one venue across the others. Multiple food courts and cafeterias can be found throughout the level, containing a mix of either decayed or decaying food, with research confirming the presence of potentially lethal strains of unknown bacteria. It is unknown how the disease transmitted from other levels, and is most likely a strain of [[Sanguine Festivus]] Multiple accounts have confirmed the presence of inexplicably warm dishes, such as noodles and pizza slices, maintaining a hot temperature despite their abandonment. It is also possible to find bottles of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]] scattered throughout the food courts and inside unlocked cars, though they are likely to be contaminated with the same unknown bacteria mentioned before, and should not be consumed. {{Blurimage|align=left|file=cafeee.png|caption=A photo of a cafeteria.}} Aside from the food stalls, clothing stores are also a common sight. Racks are generally filled with clothes from a few decades ago, with the specific era changing depending on the wanderer's age. Mannequins are scattered throughout these stores, typically in a more worn-out condition and often missing certain features. It is relatively rare to find a mannequin dressed in fashion items, and the ones who do often display tattered clothes. Multiple wanderers have reported that these mannequins appear to move closer when not observed, although most accounts of this phenomenon occur within a wanderer's later days of visiting. As '''Level 121's''' ambient temperatures remain between 4°C and 11°C (39°F to 52°F), it is advised to wear thermally protective clothing before entering, or as a last resort, to collect them from nearby clothing stores. Underneath the main levels of the mall is a parking garage, a dim space that is similarly presumed to be infinite. Empty vehicles appear in clusters, with most appearing decades old. Any wanderer attempting to operate the vehicles will discover unusable pedals and an immovable steering wheel. Despite recent modification efforts, all attempts to fix these vehicles have been deemed infeasible with current technology. The air carries a sour, metallic odor, while the walls occasionally produce muffled echoes of footsteps or engine noises without a clear source. Some areas reportedly have a more rotten fruit-like scent, despite there being no viable source of the scent. Certain sections appear more unstable, such as support pillars arranged in impossible positions, or stairwells that either lead to darkness or stop abruptly at solid walls. Wanderers have also reported that the garage's layout shifts subtly when looking away, with landmarks disappearing or reappearing in new locations. At random intervals while exploring the mall, wanderers may come across doors that lead to a cramped computer room. It consists of mold-infested wallpaper, disorganized desks, and computers resembling 1970s designs. Attempting to use these computers will result in websites related to '''Level 121''', no matter what the search query is. For instance, typing "how to escape" floods the screen with looping images of the same hallways inside the mall, while searching for "food" creates pixelated pictures of found commonly in the cafeteria. Terms like "weather" or "news" redirect to nonsensical information describing the level's environment. The search engines used are uniquely limited to this level, and usually display random symbols or glitching text. Some have claimed that certain search results return distorted audios of shows from the [[Frontrooms]]. {{Blurimage|align=right|file=computers!.png|caption=The first photo taken of the computer rooms.}} If a wanderer ventures deep enough into '''Level 121''', they will eventually reach its furthest boundaries, an unstable section where the mall's structure begins to dissolve into fragmented pieces. The environment deteriorates into an illogical area with walls flickering into existence, warped floors, and randomly positioned columns. Prolonged exposure to such a distorted location can lead to disorientation, nausea, paranoia, and eventually derealization. Wanderers who explore beyond these sections will also begin to hear a distorted melody, which gradually grows in volume until it becomes ear-damaging. Some have reported seeing similar structures to [[Level 0]] near these sections; fragments of yellow wallpaper and the scent of mold. Nonetheless, not much is known beyond brief interactions with this area, as there are only a few witness accounts to confirm its existence. Travelling anywhere beyond the level's stable boundaries is highly unrecommended as wanderers without adequate equipment will usually end up missing.
Entities
'''Level 121''' is currently classified as devoid of life. There have been no verified encounters with entities or other wanderers at the moment. It is unclear whether this is due to an isolating effect or a consequence of its infinite scale.
Recovered Notes
The following text appears to be an excerpt from the journal of an unidentified wanderer. Its origin remains unverified.
18/██/200█ Humming. A low, electric thrum beneath the floorboards, despite there being no clear power source. It drills into my head, until all coherent thoughts and memories are placed with that ''damn'' incessant noise. I used to think places like this were dead- abandoned, hollow, ''empty''. But I know better now: it's not dead. It's '''waiting'''. I don't remember my name anymore. Not clearly. I used to, back when I was wandering through that weird maze of yellow, peeling wallpaper. But not now. The edges of it slip away whenever I try to reach for it, like sand falling through my hands. It terrifies me that I'm starting to forget who I am. Sometimes I wonder if I ever was someone. Maybe I've always been here. Maybe I'm just another part of the faint melody that hums in the background, no matter where I go. The lights flicker in patterns that I ''almost'' recognize. Like Morse code, or a heartbeat. But then it'll fizzle out for more than a few seconds and I'll hold my breath until it eventually flicks back to life. Almost as if the lights are taunting me, teasing the prospect of complete darkness as I wander around aimlessly, a mere puppet waiting to be played with. I try to follow the hallways to try and find an exit to this cursed place, but stuff moves when I'm not looking. I'll turn a corner and find myself back where I started, except something is just a tiny bit different. A chair overturned. A door slightly ajar. A plate knocked over. Something's following me, watching me, ''waiting'' for me. I know it.{{Blurimage|file=recoveredimagee.png|caption=An unknown photograph found near the parking lot. Wanderer presumed deceased.}} Back when I first entered this place, I found a cafeteria filled with that disgusting food and ate a lot of it like the '''idiot''' I am. Learnt my lesson after being violently sick for a good week or two. I'm telling you, this place is mocking me, it wants me to know I'm going to die alone, slowly and painfully. How sadistic. My eyes are literally drooping close. I'm so tired. But I'm too afraid to sleep. The dreams here are worse than the waking. In them, I'm home, happy. Playing with friends, doing my homework, walking the dog, "normal people activity". But there's always something clearly ''wrong'' about them. Blurry faces, someone following me, the list goes on. I think the worst part isn't even the loneliness. It's the waiting. The creeping sensation crawling under my skin growing each day that tells me that something is bound to happen soon, that '''it''' has waited enough. ...I hope that's not true. But the longer I stay, the more it feels like I'm right. That something ''is'' '''waiting'''. Waiting for '''me'''.
The following passage was recovered from a food stall, with the date of the journal entry partially smudged out by ink and a camera found nearby with the attached image on screen.
█3/██/2006 I hate my life. At this point, I can't help but accept my fate here, to slowly die alone in an empty mall. Maybe I can find a way to jump into that black void that's beyond the windows of this place. Anyways, I've been seeing more stuff lately. I can't tell whether they're hallucinations or just this place messing with me. Figures resembling people I used to know, things I could swear used to be inside my bedroom, dumb shit like that. For the second time here, I saw her. My mother. Or something wearing her face. She stood at the end of the food court, and I caught her figure out of my peripheral vision. I called out for her, but she didn't respond. Just silently walked into a shadow between two shuttered clothing stores. When I got there, the hallways had already shifted. I just broke down in the middle of the cafeteria after that. I also decided to finally walk into those computer rooms. I was scared before of being no-clipped out of this level if I used a computer, but honestly I would rather that than dying here. I typed "escape" into one of them and the screen filled with static, then spat back a singular, flashing word: "Wait." What do you mean ''wait''? I can't wait any longer. God help me, I'm going to eat that disgusting food.
[[File:darkmall.png|400px|link=]]
Colonies and Outposts
No outposts or colonies exist in '''Level 121''', largely due to the lack of resources and potentially isolating effects. Additionally, the level's nature of shifting architecture unpredictably would inevitably cause any settlement to have to constantly rebuild over time. These factors make long-term outposts in the level impractical and unreasonable.
Entrances and Exits
===Entrances=== *Entering a dimly lit section of [[The Delta Mall]] may lead here. *Attempting to drown in the pools of [[The Neon Paradise]] has a small chance of teleporting to '''Level 121'''. *Turning a corner in [[Level 810]] may no-clip wanderers here. *Sleeping in one of the apartment bedrooms in [[Level 72]] will lead to this level. *Sliding through an orange slide in [[Level 119]]. *Random passages within [[Level 33]] can lead one here. *No-clipping through a building in [[Level 120]] may lead here. ===Exits=== Although exiting this level has been deemed almost impossible, there are a few documented methods: *Jumping off multiple floors above ground level has a chance of no-clipping one into [[The Hub]], though this should only be attempted with specific equipment. *Consuming moldy food may teleport wanderers to a food court in [[Level 36]]. *Travelling beyond the level's limits has an extremely low chance to lead one to [[Level 1000]].
